Huly Issue: VIBEK-30

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Overview

Integrate per-project Letta "PM" agents into the Huly ↔ VibeKanban sync service. Each project gets a dedicated agent with read/write capabilities via MCP servers and continuous project state updates.

## Goals

- 1 project → 1 persistent Letta agent (idempotent creation)
- Agents are read-write via MCP tools for Huly and VibeKanban
- Build PM harness that updates agent memory with project snapshots every sync
- Seed agent context with README.md (expandable later)

## Milestones

### M1: Agent Infrastructure (VIBEK-52 to VIBEK-55)

- Add database columns for Letta tracking
- Install and configure Letta Node SDK
- Create LettaService module
- Wire agent creation into sync flow

### M2: State Builder & Memory (VIBEK-56 to VIBEK-60)

- Build project metadata snapshot
- Build board metrics and hotspots analyzer
- Build backlog summary and change tracker
- Implement memory block upsert
- Integrate state builder into sync

### M3: README Upload (VIBEK-61 to VIBEK-63)

- Implement folder/source management
- Implement README upload
- Wire upload into sync flow

### M4: Optional Messaging (VIBEK-64)

- Add agent messaging with change summaries

### M5: Future Enhancements (VIBEK-65 to VIBEK-67)

- Design gated tool approval workflow
- Implement gated tool execution
- Expand document ingestion

## Success Criteria

- Each project has a dedicated PM agent
- Agents have fresh, structured Kanban state on every sync
- README uploaded to agent context
- Agents can use Huly/Vibe MCP tools (read/write)
- System is robust and idempotent

## References

- PROPOSAL_LETTA_PM_AGENT.md in repo root
- Design doc has full architecture and implementation details

## Dependencies

- Letta server at https://letta2.oculair.ca/v1
- Huly MCP server
- Vibe Kanban MCP server

---

* [ ] Persist folder_id and source_id to database
* [ ] Call uploadReadme with agent, folder, source IDs

* [ ] Respect DRY_RUN mode
* [ ] Handle failures without breaking sync
* [ ] Add performance logging

## Acceptance Criteria

- README uploaded for projects with repo paths
- Upload happens on first sync
- Subsequent syncs skip if README unchanged (optional)
- Sync continues even if upload fails

title: M2.5: Integrate state builder into sync flow

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Description

Wire the state builder functions into processProject to update agent memory on each sync.

## Tasks

- [ ] After agent ensure in processProject, build project snapshot
- [ ] Call buildProjectMeta, buildBoardConfig, buildBoardMetrics, etc.
- [ ] Collect all blocks into array
- [ ] Call LettaService.upsertMemoryBlocks(agentId, blocks)
- [ ] Update letta_last_sync_at timestamp in DB
- [ ] Add performance logging (time to build + upsert)
- [ ] Respect DRY_RUN mode
- [ ] Handle errors gracefully (don't fail entire sync)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Agent memory updated with fresh state on every sync
- DB tracks when agent was last updated
- Sync continues even if Letta update fails
- Performance stays acceptable (< 2s for memory updates)

## References

- PROPOSAL_LETTA_PM_AGENT.md lines 112-125, 160-167

Huly Issue: VIBEK-39

title: Expand MCP Server with Comprehensive Tool Coverage

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Summary

Expand the Vibe Kanban MCP server from 7 basic tools to 35+ comprehensive tools covering the complete task lifecycle and workflow management.

## Current State

**Existing Tools (7)**:

- list_projects
- list_tasks, get_task, create_task, update_task, delete_task
- start_task_attempt

**Missing Critical Functionality**:

- Task attempt management (merge, rebase, artifacts, logs)
- Executor profile discovery and management
- Execution process monitoring
- Project configuration and branch management
- Approval workflows
- And 28+ more tools

## Problem Statement

During testing, we discovered critical gaps:

1. **No executor profile discovery** - Agents can't see available executors (claude-code, opencode, etc.)
2. **No merge capability** - Had to use raw API: 
3. **No attempt history** - Can't see what was tried or why it failed
4. **No log access** - Can't debug execution failures
5. **No artifact retrieval** - Can't access work products from attempts

This limits agent autonomy and requires manual intervention for basic workflows.

## Proposed Solution

Implement comprehensive MCP tool coverage in 3 phases:

### Phase 1: Critical Gaps (HIGH Priority)

**Executor Profile Management (5 tools)**:

- list_executor_profiles, get_executor_profile
- create_executor_profile, update_executor_profile, delete_executor_profile

**Task Attempt Management (16 tools)**:

- list_task_attempts, get_task_attempt, get_attempt_artifacts
- merge_task_attempt, rebase_task_attempt, abort_conflicts
- create_followup_attempt, replace_execution_process
- get_commit_info, compare_commit_to_head, push_attempt_branch
- create_github_pr, change_target_branch, get_branch_status
- delete_attempt_file, open_attempt_in_editor

**Execution Process Management (7 tools)**:

- list_execution_processes, get_execution_process, stop_execution_process
- get_process_raw_logs, get_process_normalized_logs
- start_dev_server, stream_process_logs

### Phase 2: Enhanced Discovery (MEDIUM Priority)

**Project Management (8 tools)**:

- get_project, create_project, update_project, delete_project
- get_project_branches, search_project_files
- open_project_in_editor, get_project_executor_profiles

**Task Enhancements (6 tools)**:

- create_task_and_start, get_task_images, upload_task_image
- search_tasks, bulk_update_tasks, get_task_history

**System Configuration (8 tools)**:

- get_system_info, get_config, update_config
- list_mcp_servers, update_mcp_servers
- list_git_repos, list_directory, health_check

### Phase 3: Workflow Features (LOW Priority)

**Approval Workflow (4 tools)**, **Tags (5 tools)**, **GitHub Auth (3 tools)**

## Technical Approach

**Location**: Extend 

**Pattern** (existing code style):



All API endpoints already exist in 

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 35+ tools implemented and tested
- [ ] Complete task lifecycle possible without UI
- [ ] Agents can discover executors, projects, branches
- [ ] Error handling and validation prevent bad states
- [ ] Documentation updated with all new tools
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Benefits

1. **Agent Autonomy**: Complete workflows without human intervention
2. **Debugging**: Access to logs and attempt history
3. **Iterative Development**: Follow-up attempts addressing feedback
4. **Discoverability**: Agents can explore available resources
5. **Reliability**: Proper error handling and state management

## Estimated Effort

- Phase 1: 40-60 hours
- Phase 2: 30-40 hours
- Phase 3: 20-30 hours

Total: ~100-130 hours (12-16 days)

## References

- Full specification: 
- Tested merge endpoint:  ✅
- Backend API: 
- Meridian consultation: Provided critical workflow insights

---

comments.author: node
comments.text:
## Description

Implement system to intercept agent tool calls and gate them with human approval.

## Tasks

- [ ] Create approval queue in database
- [ ] Implement tool call interceptor/middleware
- [ ] Build approval UI/CLI/Slack integration (based on M5.1 design)
- [ ] Implement approval persistence and audit log
- [ ] Add timeout handling (auto-reject after N hours)
- [ ] Implement approved action execution
- [ ] Add rejection feedback loop to agent
- [ ] Create dashboard for pending approvals

## Acceptance Criteria

- Agent tool calls intercepted correctly
- Approvals flow through chosen channel
- Approved actions execute successfully
- Rejected actions provide feedback to agent
- Full audit trail maintained
